plot_beats.beat_text: Wide establishing shot of the garden behind the old house. Dawn light, stillness before the day.
Hana appears in silhouette against the garden behind the old house. Their posture tells a story before any word is spoken.
Close on Hana's hands — fidgeting, gripping, or still. The body knows what the mind hasn't admitted.
Hana walks through the familiar space. Everything looks the same. Something has shifted.
A small detail catches Hana's eye — a crack in the wall, a wilting plant, a clock stopped at the wrong time.
the memory of Mother enters the frame without warning. Hana stiffens.
The first exchange. Words say one thing. Eyes say another. Neither acknowledges the gap.
Hana alone again. They exhale. The room holds the echo.
A memory surfaces — not as flashback but as body sensation. Hana's shoulders rise.
Night falls over the garden behind the old house. Hana at the window. The reflection stares back.
SILENCE: The world breathes. No dialogue. No action. Just the space between heartbeats.
Hana makes a small choice — turns left instead of right, picks up a forgotten object, opens a closed door.
Hana faces the memory of Mother across a charged space. Neither moves first.
Close on Hana's face as realization hits — the pattern they've been blind to.
Hana tries the old response. It doesn't work anymore. The body rejects the habit.
A confrontation builds. the memory of Mother pushes. Hana almost breaks — then catches themselves.
Hana alone, processing. The walls feel closer. Or maybe they always were this close.
An unexpected kindness from a stranger. Hana doesn't know how to receive it.
Hana speaks truth for the first time — halting, imperfect, real.
The cost of the truth lands. Silence follows. Neither person looks away.
Hana walks a new path. Each step deliberate. The ground feels different.
SILENCE: A breath held and released. The world continues. Something has loosened.
the memory of Mother shows a crack in their armor. For one panel, the shadow looks human.
Hana returns to a familiar place but sees it differently. The change is internal.
Hana and the memory of Mother face each other for the final time. This isn't about winning.
Everything Hana has learned crystallizes in one moment. The body moves before the mind.
Hana chooses the harder path — not the dramatic one, but the honest one.
the memory of Mother falters. Not defeated — seen. The shadow recognized is no longer a shadow.
The world holds still. What needed to happen has happened. What needed to break has broken.
SILENCE: The deepest pause. Three panels of nothing but breath and light.
Hana stands in the aftermath. Changed not by victory but by surrender.
A hand extended — to the memory of Mother, to self, to the reader. Integration, not triumph.
Hana speaks the last words of the arc. Quiet. Unforced. True.
the garden behind the old house at peace. The light has changed. Dawn or dusk — it doesn't matter. What matters is the stillness.
Final panel: Hana's eyes. Not resolved. Not healed. Awake.
SILENCE: The story ends where the reader begins. No summary. No lesson. Just the echo.
